records.ety: [L. intelligens, intellegens, -entis, p. pr. of intelligere, intellegere, to perceive; inter between + legere to gather, collect, choose: cf. F. intelligent. See Legend.]

defs: Endowed with the faculty of understanding or reason; as, man is an intelligent being.

Possessed of a high level of intelligence, education, or judgment; knowing; sensible; skilled; exhibiting high intelligence; as, an intelligent young man; an intelligent architect; an intelligent answer.

quotes: Which are to France the spies and speculations Intelligent of our state.
